headline: Foreign Worker Health Levy System Excludes 1.8 Million From Care
context: Documented foreign workers pay mandatory health levies but access to public healthcare remains restricted and confusing.

cards.big: 1.8 million documented foreign workers pay health levies but face restricted hospital access
cards.sub: Foreign worker surcharge of 150% on public hospital fees deters healthcare seeking

cards.big: FOMEMA collects RM420 million annually in foreign worker health screening fees
cards.sub: Revenue goes to general government coffers not to funding healthcare access for contributors

cards.big: Untreated TB among foreign workers accounts for 38% of Malaysia's TB disease burden
cards.sub: Restricting healthcare access for workers creates public health risk for entire population

cards.big: Singapore's CMIO healthcare system provides subsidized care to all work permit holders
cards.sub: Inclusive health coverage protects both migrant workers and host country population

cards.big: Charging people who already paid levies 150% more for hospital care is double taxation not public health
Should foreign worker health levies entitle them to standard public healthcare access
